content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File -
R~ T. Denny, Registrar of Voters, 
San Diego, Letter of 8/29/78 
Date 
10/78 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Jay Greenwood 
Subject: 
Informed Keith Boyer, R. T. Denny's replacement, 
by phone that my original advice was correct. 
In addition, 
I advised that the statement required to be filed with 
the city need only disclose receipts and expenditures in 
connection with the city, if any. 


August 29, 1978 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
I would appreciate clarification of recent oral advice given by John Greenwood 
to the San Diego City Clerk. 
Councilman Tom Gade was a candidate for Municipal Court Judge in the June 6, 
1978, Primary Election and filed Consolidated Form 490 Campaign Statements 
three times as required. 
He has no committees as a councilman. 
This office 
informed him that he need not file an "off year" statement for the period 
ending June 30, 1978, because of the wording of Section 84206: 
"Every 
candidate and committee that receives contributions or makes expenditures 
during the periods specified in this section, and every elected officer 
except as provided below, shall file campaign statements as provided in 
this section, unless the candidate, committee or elected officer is required 
to file cam ai n statements in connection with an 
election or elections 
held within the periods specified in subsections a and 
In a telephone conversation with the City Clerk's Office, Mr. Greenwood 
stated that Councilman Gade was also required to file an "off year" campaign 
statement with the San Diego City Clerk. 
This is not a request for an opinion. 
Your commission has stated previously 
that filing officers lack standing in the matter of requesting opinions. 
Because local candidates rely on this office for information and advice, I 
would like to give them the correct information. 
R. 1. DENNY 
Registrar of Voters 
RTD:YS:hl

sections.question: Clarification of recent oral advice given by John Greenwood to the San Diego City Clerk regarding whether Councilman Tom Gade, a candidate for Municipal Court Judge in the June 6, 1978 Primary Election who filed Consolidated Form 490 Campaign Statements three times as required, must file an 'off year' statement for the period ending June 30, 1978 with the San Diego City Clerk, given Section 84206's language regarding required campaign statements.
sections.conclusion: Informed Keith Boyer, R. T. Denny's replacement, by phone that my original advice was correct. In addition, I advised that the statement required to be filed with the city need only disclose receipts and expenditures in connection with the city, if any.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate for judicial office who filed required campaign statements for an election must also file an 'off year' campaign statement with the city, and clarification of conflicting oral advice on this matter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the original advice was correct and clarified that any statement filed with the city need only disclose receipts and expenditures connected to city matters.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum to file dated 10/78 with an attached letter dated 8/29/78. The question is reconstructed from the incoming letter from R.T. Denny. The conclusion is brief and appears to be a summary of phone advice given. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present; content extracted from body text. Confidence reduced due to fragmented nature of advice (oral communication to replacement) and limited detail in the formal response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Clarification of recent oral advice given by John Greenwood to the San Diego City Clerk regarding whether Councilman Tom Gade, a candidate for Municipal Court Judge in the June 6, 1978 Primary Election who filed Consolidated Form 490 Campaign Statements three times as required, must file an 'off year' statement for the period ending June 30, 1978 with the San Diego City Clerk, given Section 84206's language regarding required campaign statements.

CONCLUSION: Informed Keith Boyer, R. T. Denny's replacement, by phone that my original advice was correct. In addition, I advised that the statement required to be filed with the city need only disclose receipts and expenditures in connection with the city, if any.

embedding.summary: The Registrar of Voters sought clarification regarding conflicting FPPC oral advice about whether a municipal council candidate who ran for judicial office must file an 'off year' campaign statement with the city. The FPPC confirmed its original advice and clarified the scope of required local filings.
